title: grammosolen odgersii subsp. occidentalis
intro: Grammosolen odgersii subsp. occidentalis, also known as a type of yam, is a plant species native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Grammosolen odgersii subsp. occidentalis are typically made from the root and can be used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
